name: Fly.io
what_it_does: Fly.io is a platform for running containerized applications globally close to users, using their Machines API (REST) and flyctl CLI. It runs Docker containers as fast-booting Fly Machines (micro-VMs based on Firecracker) across 30+ regions, with built-in Anycast networking, Postgres, Redis, and object storage.
use_cases: Deploying agent containers to multiple global regions with a single API call for low-latency access
Using the Fly Machines API to programmatically spin up and tear down ephemeral agent workers on demand
Running agent sidecars or microservices close to users via Fly's Anycast edge network
Hosting long-running agent backends on always-on Fly Machines with automatic restart policies
Scaling agent workloads horizontally by creating multiple Machines via the REST API
Running scheduled agent tasks using Fly's built-in cron and scheduled machine stop/start
not_for: Teams that need traditional Kubernetes orchestration with full cluster control
Serverless compute that scales to zero in milliseconds — Fly Machines have a slightly longer cold start
Managed databases with full RDS-style operational support
Enterprise workloads needing dedicated compliance infrastructure or private cloud deployment
best_when: You want simpler-than-Kubernetes container deployment with global edge presence, programmatic machine management via REST API, and integrated Postgres/Redis — without managing your own infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need Kubernetes-native features (Helm, operators, CRDs), require your own cloud account (Fly manages the underlying hardware), or need enterprise SLAs with dedicated account management.

auth.notes: Fly API tokens are organization-scoped. Tokens are created via flyctl or the Fly web dashboard. Deploy tokens can be scoped to a single app for CI/CD. No OAuth2 or fine-grained resource scoping beyond app-level deploy tokens. Tokens are bearer tokens sent in Authorization header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free allowance: 3 shared-CPU-1x VMs with 256MB RAM, 160GB outbound bandwidth/month, 3GB persistent volumes
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go: shared-CPU-1x at ~$1.94/month; dedicated CPU VMs from ~$5.70/month; GPU machines from $0.75/hour (A10). No minimum spend.
pricing.notes: Credit card required to deploy beyond the free allowance, even if you stay within free limits. Free allowance is per-organization. Pricing is transparent and usage-based with no surprise minimums.

performance.rate_limits: Machines API: 100 requests/second per organization. No hard limits on app traffic — governed by your machine resources.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Machines API returns structured JSON errors with status codes and message fields. Error messages are generally clear for common failures (image not found, capacity exhausted). Some edge cases return generic 500 errors without detail.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Machine creation is not idempotent by default — calling create twice creates two machines. Agents must track machine IDs to avoid duplication. App creation is idempotent if same app name used.
known_agent_gotchas: Machines API and the older Apps API (GraphQL) are separate — use Machines API for programmatic control
Machine boot time (Firecracker microVM) is typically 300-500ms — plan for cold starts in agent workflows
Fly Volumes (persistent storage) are single-region only — cannot be shared across regions
Private networking between machines uses WireGuard — requires .internal DNS, not public IP addresses
Fly Postgres is deployed as a Fly app, not a managed database — you own operations and backups
Credit card required even to use free tier machines after initial setup
Deploy tokens are app-scoped — agents managing multiple apps need multiple tokens or an org token

security_score_components.security_notes: Fly secrets are stored encrypted and injected as environment variables. WireGuard mesh provides encrypted private networking between machines. App-level deploy tokens limit blast radius for CI/CD. TLS termination handled automatically at the Fly edge.
